title: Manta ray
content:
{{Top|leg|notoc=1}}
'''Manta rays''' were, like [[Jellyfish/Legends|jellyfish]] and the [[Portuguese man-o'-war]], creatures that closely resembled the [[Oswaft]] of the [[ThonBoka]] nebula. They were broad and streamlined, powerfully winged, sinister in appearance and yet pleasing to look at, and their dorsal surfaces were domed with high muscle.<ref name="thonboka">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]''</ref> The [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems's]] [[Manta droid subfighter]] took its name from the manta ray,<ref name="db">{{DB|droid|mantasubfighter|manta droid subfighter}}</ref> and the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Keelkana-class attack submersible/Legends|''Keelkana''-class attack submersible]] also took inspiration from the creature.<ref name="strongholds">''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]''</ref>

[[Mynock/Legends|Mynocks]]<ref name="Breakout">{{WEGCite|story=Rebel Breakout|book=Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)}}</ref> and [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Thranta/Legends|thrantas]] were often described as looking like giant manta-rays.<ref name="ccw">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
A manta ray appears in the [[LEGO]] set [[W:c:brickipedia:7161 Gungan Sub|7161 Gungan Sub]]. This serves as the first visual depiction of them, but is [[Canon|non-canon]].<ref name="LEGOGunganSub">{{LEGOCite|num=7161|name=Gungan Sub}}</ref>

*''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' {{1stm}}
